description_long: This is a weathered, black ceramic bucket with a wide, flared rim and a single rounded handle attached to one side. The surface is matte and uneven, showing clear signs of age, wear, and multiple repairs, with visible cracks and areas where the material has been rejoined. The bucket is missing several pieces, especially along the lower edge, resulting in large gaps and an incomplete base. The interior is rough and unfinished, with the same dark coloration as the exterior, and the handle appears to be formed from the same ceramic material as the body. There are no visible decorative elements, labels, or markings, giving it a utilitarian appearance. This object would likely be found in an outdoor or archaeological urban context, possibly used for carrying or holding materials.
description: A black, weathered ceramic bucket with a wide rim, single rounded handle, and visible cracks and missing sections, giving it a rough, aged appearance.
description_view_0: The bucket is seen from an angle highlighting the wide rim, large missing section at the base, and the handle on the right side.
description_view_1: This view shows the handle more centrally, with cracks and repairs visible along the rim and body, and a smaller missing section at the base.
description_view_2: The handle is on the left, and a large missing section at the rim is visible, with the bucket's rough, patched surface prominent.
description_view_3: The view focuses on the largest missing section at the base, with the handle not visible and the rim appearing mostly intact.
